event: High-energy laser C-UAS systems create demand spike for targeting sensors and beam directors — FAA-DoD border agreement enables domestic deployment beyond test ranges
evidence_basis: DefenseNews 2026-04-13: FAA and DoD reached 'landmark safety agreement' for high-energy laser weapons deployment at US-Mexico border after safety assessment. TWZ 2026-04-10: Pentagon-FAA signed agreement following New Mexico testing validation. Previous Feb 25 incident where military errantly shot down government drone indicates systems already in field testing.
probability_basis: Agreement already signed, testing complete, two incidents confirm systems in use — deployment acceleration is verified policy outcome
drivers: FAA-DoD landmark safety agreement signed April 2026
Validated safety controls after New Mexico laser testing
Low-cost drone threat driving domestic air defense requirements

lead_time_signal.component: High-power laser diodes and beam control optics

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Laser C-UAS systems require integrated EW for target discrimination and airspace deconfliction

event: Hezbollah FPV drone ramp against IDF armor validates ESC and flight controller supply pressure — combat footage shows Chinese-origin components still dominant in adversary systems
evidence_basis: TWZ 2026-04-10: 'Hezbollah is increasing its first-person view (FPV) drone strikes on IDF armor and personnel... has ramped up the attacks since 2024... small, fast and easy to maneuver weapons has proliferated from the war in Ukraine to battlefields across the globe.' Parts DB shows ESCs: 72% China-origin (119/165), FPV cameras: 56% China-origin (187/336).
probability_basis: Direct combat usage confirmed by TWZ reporting; parts DB verifies China dominates commercial FPV supply chain; proliferation pattern from Ukraine to Lebanon is documented
drivers: Hezbollah ramping FPV strikes per TWZ April 2026 reporting
Commercial Chinese ESC/FC components dominate adversary supply chains

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: FPV kamikaze tactics against armor require 6-8 week training pipeline from Ukraine conflict documentation

event: RBAF F-16 Block 70 scores first air-to-air kills against Iranian drones — validates integrated counter-UAS radar and missile inventory drawdown
evidence_basis: TWZ 2026-04-10: 'Bahrain has scored the first air-to-air kills by the latest Block 70 variant of the iconic F-16 Viper, taking down two Iranian drones during the conflict in the Middle East earlier this month.' Royal Bahraini Air Force F-16D Block 70 confirmed kills. Article notes 'air forces around the world grapple with the emerging aerial drone threat.'
probability_basis: TWZ confirmed RBAF F-16 Block 70 kills earlier in April 2026 — verified combat event, not projection; signals tactical doctrine shift using manned fighters against low-cost drones
drivers: RBAF F-16 Block 70 downed two Iranian drones April 2026 per TWZ
First combat kills for Block 70 variant validate sensor/missile integration
Cost-exchange problem: $20M fighter firing $500K missiles at $20K drones

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Manned fighter intercepts of low-cost drones signal doctrine gaps — drives lower-cost interceptor demand (Coyote, Alta Ares)

event: Ukrainian defense firms' Paris meetings with French counterparts accelerate European co-production — joint ventures lag Germany (11) and Spain (5) at only 1 for France
evidence_basis: DefenseNews 2026-04-10: 'More than two dozen Ukrainian defense companies traveled to Paris this week to meet with French counterparts, laying the groundwork for co-production deals in France.' Ihor Fedirko (CEO Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry) stated 'just one joint venture created so far [with France], compared to 11 for Germany and five for Spain.' Meeting occurred late Wednesday after day of meetings.
probability_basis: Meetings confirmed April 10; France significantly lags Germany/Spain in joint ventures (1 vs 11 vs 5) — catching up is stated policy goal; H2 timeframe reflects normal industrial partnership negotiation cycles
drivers: Two dozen Ukrainian defense firms met French counterparts in Paris April 10
France has only 1 joint venture vs Germany's 11 and Spain's 5
Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry CEO explicitly seeking French co-production deals

lead_time_signal.component: European-sourced flight controllers and ESCs from joint ventures

event: Validation: USS Carl M. Levin mystery launcher likely Coyote counter-drone system expansion — USS Bainbridge and USS Winston S. Churchill already equipped, signals Navy-wide interceptor integration
evidence_basis: TWZ 2026-04-10: 'A new and not immediately recognizable launcher has emerged on the U.S. Navy's Arleigh Burke class destroyer USS Carl M. Levin. Last year, launchers for Coyote counter-drone interceptors appeared in the same general location on two other Burkes, the USS Bainbridge and the USS Winston S. Churchill.' Article states Navy 'exploring several other options for integrating lower-cost anti-drone interceptors on various warships.'
probability_basis: Two Burkes already have Coyote launchers in same location; Carl M. Levin launcher location match suggests pattern replication; Navy stated policy is integrating lower-cost interceptors fleet-wide
drivers: USS Carl M. Levin launcher matches USS Bainbridge/USS Winston S. Churchill Coyote locations
Navy explicitly exploring lower-cost anti-drone interceptor integration
Arleigh Burke class standardization enables rapid cross-hull deployment

lead_time_signal.component: Coyote interceptor airframes and tube-launch systems

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Navy investing in quantity-based interceptor defense against drone swarms — validates Semtech LoRa and mesh radio demand for coordination
